1.1: In the second year of the reign of Darius (lord), on the first day of the sixth month, the word of the LORD {YHWH} came through Haggai (festive) the prophet to Zerubbabel (sown in Babylon) son of Shealtiel (I have asked of God), governor of Judah (praised), and to Joshua (YHWH saves) son of Jehozadak (Jozadak), the high priest, stating
1.2: that this is what the LORD {YHWH} of Hosts says: “These people say, ‘The time has not yet come to rebuild the house of the LORD.
1.3: Then the word of the LORD {YHWH} came through Haggai (festive) the prophet, saying:
1.4: “Is it a time for you? yourselves to live in your? paneled houses, while this house lies in ruins?
1.5: Now this is what the LORD {YHWH} of Hosts says: “Consider carefully your? ways.
1.6: You? have planted much but harvested little. You eat but never have enough. You drink but never have your fill. You put on clothes but never get warm. You earn wages to put into a bag pierced through.
1.7: This is what the LORD {YHWH} of Hosts says: “Consider carefully your? ways.
1.8: Go up into the hills, bring down lumber, and build the house, so that I may take pleasure in it and be glorified, says the LORD {YHWH}.
1.9: You expected much, but behold, it amounted to little. And what you? brought home, I blew away. Why? declares the LORD {YHWH} of Hosts. Because My house still lies in ruins, while each of you? is busy with his own house.
1.10: Therefore, on account of you? the heavens have withheld their dew and the earth has withheld its crops.
1.11: I have summoned a drought on the fields and on the mountains, on the grain, new wine, and oil, and on whatever the ground yields, on man and beast, and on all the labor of your hands.
1.12: Then Zerubbabel (sown in Babylon) son of Shealtiel (I have asked of God) and Joshua (YHWH saves) son of Jehozadak (YHWH is righteous), the high priest, as well as all the remnant of the people, obeyed the voice of the LORD their God {YHWH Elohehem} and the words of the prophet Haggai (festive), because the LORD their God had sent him. So the people feared the LORD.
1.13: Haggai (festive), the messenger of the LORD {YHWH}, delivered the message of the LORD to the people: “I am with you?, declares the LORD.
1.14: So the LORD {YHWH} stirred the spirit {ruach} of Zerubbabel (sown in Babylon) son of Shealtiel (I have asked of God), governor of Judah (praised), and the spirit of Joshua (YHWH saves) son of Jehozadak (YHWH is righteous), the high priest, as well as the spirit of all the remnant of the people. And they came and began the work on the house of the LORD of Hosts, their God {Elohehem},
1.15: on the twenty-fourth day of the sixth month, in the second year of King Darius (lord).

2.1: On the twenty-first day of the seventh month, the word of the LORD {YHWH} came through Haggai (festive) the prophet, saying:
2.2: “Speak to Zerubbabel (sown in Babylon) son of Shealtiel (I have asked of God), governor of Judah (praised), and to Joshua (YHWH saves) son of Jehozadak (Jozadak), the high priest, and also to the remnant of the people. Ask them,
2.3: ‘Who is left among you? who saw this house in its former glory? How does it look to you? now? Does it not appear to you? like nothing in comparison?
2.4: But now be strong, O Zerubbabel (sown in Babylon), declares the LORD {YHWH}. Be strong, O Joshua (YHWH saves) son of Jehozadak (YHWH is righteous), the high priest. And be strong, all you people of the land, declares the LORD. Work! For I am with you?, declares the LORD of Hosts.
2.5: This is the promise I made to you? when you? came out of Egypt (land of bondage). And My Spirit {Ruachi} remains among you?; do not be afraid.
2.6: For this is what the LORD {YHWH} of Hosts says: “Once more, in a little while, I will shake the heavens and the earth , the sea and the dry land.
2.7: I will shake all the nations, and they will come with all their treasures, and I will fill this house with glory, says the LORD {YHWH} of Hosts.
2.8: The silver is Mine, and the gold is Mine, declares the LORD {YHWH} of Hosts.
2.9: The latter glory of this house will be greater than the former, says the LORD {YHWH} of Hosts. And in this place I will provide peace, declares the LORD of Hosts.
2.10: On the twenty-fourth day of the ninth month, in the second year of Darius (lord), the word of the LORD {YHWH} came to Haggai (festive) the prophet, saying,
2.11: “This is what the LORD {YHWH} of Hosts says: ‘Ask the priests for a ruling.
2.12: If a man carries consecrated meat in the fold of his garment, and it touches bread, stew, wine, oil, or any other food, does that item become holy? “No, replied the priests.
2.13: So Haggai (festive) asked, “If one who is defiled by contact with a corpse touches any of these, does it become defiled? “Yes, it becomes defiled, the priests answered.
2.14: Then Haggai (festive) replied, “So it is with this people and this nation before Me, declares the LORD {YHWH}, and so it is with every work of their hands; whatever they offer there is defiled.
2.15: Now consider carefully from this day forward (backward): Before one stone was placed on another in the temple of the LORD {YHWH},
2.16: from that time, when one came expecting a heap of twenty ephahs of grain \a heap of twenty/, there were but ten. When one came to the winepress to draw out fifty baths \to draw out fifty from the winepress/, there were but twenty.
2.17: I struck you?—all the work of your? hands—with blight, mildew, and hail, but you? did not turn to Me, declares the LORD {YHWH}.
2.18: Consider carefully from this day forward—from the twenty-fourth day of the ninth month, the day the foundation of the LORD’s {YHWH} temple was laid—consider carefully:
2.19: Is there still seed in the barn? The vine, the fig, the pomegranate, and the olive tree have not yet yielded fruit. But from this day on, I will bless you.
2.20: For the second time that day, the twenty-fourth day of the month, the word of the LORD {YHWH} came to Haggai (festive), saying,
2.21: “Tell Zerubbabel (sown in Babylon) governor of Judah (praised) that I am about to shake the heavens and the earth:
2.22: I will overturn royal thrones and destroy the power of the kingdoms of the nations. I will overturn chariots and their riders; horses and their riders will fall, each by the sword of his brother.
2.23: On that day, declares the LORD {YHWH} of Hosts, I will take you, My servant, Zerubbabel (sown in Babylon) son of Shealtiel (I have asked of God), declares the LORD, and I will make you like My signet ring, for I have chosen you, declares the LORD of Hosts.
